seo.title: Building Rails Hotwire and Turbo patterns with Rails 8 an...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Rails Hotwire and Turbo patterns with Rails 8 and Hotwire.

content.intro: This guide provides a step-by-step implementation strategy for modernizing a Rails application with Hotwire, deploying with Kamal, and integrating background job processing. Focuses on practical architecture decisions and production constraints.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of Turbo Frames and Stimulus controllers

steps.title: Audit application for Hotwire compatibility
steps.explanation: Identify JavaScript dependencies and Turbo Frame usage patterns. Check for jQuery plugins that require replacement with Stimulus controllers or Hotwire behaviors.

code.content:
grep -r 'jquery' app/javascript/packs
rails generate stimulus:controller turbo
rails generate stimulus:controller hotwire

steps.title: Update Gemfile for Rails 8 compatibility
steps.explanation: Replace Rails 7 gems with Rails 8 equivalents. Update Active Job backend to use Solid Queue for background processing.
pitfalls: Ensure all gems in Gemfile.lock are compatible with Ruby 3.1+
Check for deprecated Active Record query methods

steps.title: Configure Kamal deployment pipeline
steps.explanation: Create production deployment configuration with Kamal. Set up environment variables for database connections and secret keys.

code.content:
production:
  app: my-rails-app
  repo: git@github.com:username/my-rails-app.git
  branch: main
  secret_key_base: ENV['SECRET_KEY_BASE']

steps.title: Implement Turbo Streams for real-time updates
steps.explanation: Replace AJAX calls with Turbo Streams. Create partials for DOM updates and add Turbo Stream tags to views.

steps.title: Set up Sidekiq with Redis for background jobs
steps.explanation: Configure Sidekiq middleware and Redis connection. Add job classes to Active Job queue with proper retry policies.

code.content:
class CommentNotificationJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default
  retry_on StandardError, wait: 5.seconds, attempts: 3
  def perform(comment_id)
    CommentMailer.notify(comment_id).deliver_now
  end
end

pitfalls: Ensure Redis connection pool size matches concurrent job processing needs
Avoid long-running jobs in default queue

steps.title: Create system tests with RSpec and FactoryBot
steps.explanation: Write integration tests for Hotwire interactions. Use FactoryBot to create test data for complex scenarios.

code.content:
FactoryBot.define do
  factory :comment do
    body { 'Test comment' }
    post
  end
end

content.conclusion: Verify all Hotwire components work without JavaScript, confirm Kamal deployment process, and ensure background jobs process reliably. Run full test suite with coverage report before production release.
